opp.name: Wanzl_srm_sourcing 2026
opp.next_step: Jun 22, 2026 VP S. Schmidt is currently ooo. Neha to schedule follow up meetings with Manuel. Schedule a 3–4 hour deep-dive with Manuel, Siegfried, operational procurement, and IT. The session should focus on supplier onboarding, RFX/auctions, operational purchasing, and integration across Wanzl’s 11 ERP systems . Zycus should also use this meeting to validate fit around AI-led sourcing, contract renewals, Teams-based intake, and SAP transition support .
opp.ais_why:
* AI capabilities were rep-presented within an active evaluation: Zycus highlighted "AI-driven autonomous sourcing, contract renewal, and supplier management agents" (e.g., Heineken autonomous sourcing up to EUR 20,000) and a Teams "digital assistant."
* But the customer's stated priorities are integration and scope, not AI: a middleware to connect "11 ERP systems," supplier onboarding/sourcing/operational purchasing across ~20 countries, and concerns about "direct material procurement" coverage.
* It is a structured, methodical selection (screening mid-year, deep dives, year-end "beauty contest," project start late 2026); AI is one capability under evaluation, not a voiced driver. Medium confidence.

items.quote: Wanzl's current three-pillar scope prioritizes direct/operational procurement. Zycus does NOT handle direct-material production planning, 8D quality reports, or product-level document management/technical drawings. Must validate in deep-dive session that RFX/supplier-onboarding modules are generic enough for direct-spend workflows, or position best-of-breed approach and acknowledge trade-off.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 18 May 2026 (scope discussion) + known Zycus product limitations

sources: Avoma discovery call 18 May 2026, Manuel Kehle (Expert Methods & Processes)
Next Step 22 Jun 2026 (deep-dive agenda includes 'supplier onboarding, RFX/auctions, operational purchasing, 11-ERP integration')
metrics.narrative: Wanzl articulated operational pain around fragmented procurement (11 ERP systems, no unified RFX/auction platform, manual supplier onboarding across 20 countries) on the 18 May discovery call. Manuel Kehle stated 'we have no single source-to-contract system, every region runs its own process, and we lose negotiation leverage because we cannot consolidate RFX across geographies.' No quantified savings target, headcount reduction goal, or cycle-time improvement articulated yet. The pain is confirmed and operationally credible (multi-ERP complexity is real), but the business case remains qualitative. Quantifying the value case (cost of fragmentation, savings potential, ROI timeline) is the critical next move for the deep-dive session.

sources: Avoma discovery call 18 May 2026, Manuel Kehle (engaged, validated scope)
Salesforce Contact Roles: Manuel Kehle (Evaluator), Siegfried Schmidt (VP Group Purchasing, Decision Maker, never engaged)
Next Step 22 Jun 2026: 'VP S. Schmidt is currently ooo. Neha to schedule follow up meetings with Manuel.' (no buyer confirmation yet)
champion.narrative: Manuel Kehle (Expert Methods & Processes, Evaluator role) is the only actively engaged contact and is developing as a champion. On the 18 May discovery call he articulated Wanzl's pain, validated the iSource/iSupplier/Merlin scope, and agreed to coordinate the next deep-dive session. However, he has not demonstrated active advocacy (no internal introductions, no competitive pushback, no urgency signaling) and has not confirmed the deep-dive scheduling despite the 22 Jun rep request (7 days ago, no reply). His engagement is cooling (42 days since last contact). He does not have direct access to the Economic Buyer (EB unmapped) or decision authority (Evaluator role, not Decision Maker). Siegfried Schmidt (VP Group Purchasing, Decision Maker) is listed in Contact Roles but has never engaged directly and is currently out-of-office. Champion development is weak and at risk: Manuel is passive, single-threaded, and has not driven momentum. Strengthening Manuel's advocacy and engaging Siegfried Schmidt are critical next moves.

sources: Salesforce Competitors__c: empty
Avoma discovery call 18 May 2026: no competitor mentioned
Next Step 22 Jun 2026: 'year-end beauty contest' implies multi-vendor evaluation, no names
competition.narrative: Competition is unknown. No competitor named in Salesforce Competitors__c field, no competitive mentions on the 18 May discovery call, and no competitive intel in Next Step or Tasks. The only indirect signal is the 'year-end beauty contest' reference on 18 May, confirming Wanzl is evaluating multiple vendors in parallel, but no rival names (SAP Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua, or others) have been surfaced. For a formal RFP with a shortlist gate, competitive intel is critical. The deep-dive session and follow-up calls must surface who else is in the running, their positioning, and Wanzl's perception of each vendor. Until then, the competitive read is a blind spot.

sources: Avoma discovery call 18 May 2026, Manuel Kehle
Salesforce Contact Roles: Siegfried Schmidt (VP Group Purchasing, Decision Maker), Manuel Kehle (Evaluator)
identify_pain.narrative: Wanzl's operational pain is fragmented procurement infrastructure: 11 separate ERP systems across 20 countries with no unified RFX, auction, or supplier-onboarding platform. Manuel Kehle stated on 18 May, 'we have no single source-to-contract system, every region runs its own process, and we lose negotiation leverage because we cannot consolidate RFX across geographies.' The pain owner is Group Purchasing (Siegfried Schmidt, VP Group Purchasing, Decision Maker role) with operational execution led by Manuel Kehle (Expert Methods & Processes). The pain is confirmed and operationally credible (multi-ERP complexity, regional fragmentation, lost leverage), but not yet quantified (no savings target, headcount impact, or cycle-time reduction articulated).

sources: Next Step 22 Jun 2026 ('SAP transition support' mentioned, implying SAP contract transition)
Salesforce: no Current_Contract_Expiration__c, no contracting or legal fields populated
paper_process.narrative: Paper process (MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec, SI/partner involvement) is unknown. No contracting mechanics, legal timeline, or InfoSec requirements discussed on the 18 May call or documented in Salesforce. No SI or implementation partner mentioned. No Current Contract Expiration date recorded (Salesforce field empty). The only indirect signal is the 'SAP transition support' scope in the Next Step agenda, implying an active SAP migration that may create a forcing function or timeline dependency, but the contract-expiration date and procurement process are not confirmed. The deep-dive session should surface InfoSec requirements, contracting timeline, and any SI involvement.

sources: Salesforce Contact Roles: Siegfried Schmidt (VP Group Purchasing, Decision Maker) listed but not yet engaged
Avoma discovery call 18 May 2026: Manuel Kehle mentioned 'steering board' and 'Group Purchasing' but no EB named
Next Step 22 Jun 2026: 'VP S. Schmidt is currently ooo' (no EB engagement attempt)
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is unmapped. No CFO, CIO, Group CPO, or C-level executive identified or engaged in Salesforce Contact Roles, call history, or Next Step. For a €200k, multi-year, 20-country platform initiative requiring steering-board approval and international content-team coordination (per 18 May call governance structure), the EB must control budget authority and sign-off. The 18 May call referenced a 'steering board' and 'Group Purchasing oversight' but no individual was named. VP Siegfried Schmidt (Decision Maker, VP Group Purchasing) is the most senior known contact but has been out-of-office since mid-June with no direct engagement yet. Mapping and engaging the EB is critical before the year-end beauty-contest shortlist gate.

sources: Avoma discovery call 18 May 2026, Manuel Kehle (mentioned 'year-end beauty contest' and 'steering board')
Salesforce Contact Roles: Manuel Kehle (Evaluator), Siegfried Schmidt (Decision Maker)
Next Step 22 Jun 2026 (deep-dive with Manuel, Siegfried, operational procurement, IT)
decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal RFP process with a year-end 2026 'beauty contest' shortlist gate (mentioned on 18 May call). Manuel Kehle (Expert Methods & Processes, Evaluator role) is leading initial discovery and vendor evaluation. Siegfried Schmidt (VP Group Purchasing, Decision Maker role) oversees the process but has not engaged directly yet (out-of-office as of 22 Jun). The 18 May call referenced a 'steering board' for final approval and an 'international content team' for 20-country rollout, indicating a multi-layer approval chain, but the process map (stages, gates, timelines, approvers) is not yet documented. The next milestone is the cross-functional deep-dive (operational procurement, IT, Group Purchasing) to validate technical fit and advance to the shortlist. No buyer-owned process timeline or RFP response deadline has been shared yet.

sources: Avoma discovery call 18 May 2026, Manuel Kehle

Next Step 22 Jun 2026 (deep-dive agenda: 'supplier onboarding, RFX/auctions, operational purchasing, 11-ERP integration, AI-led sourcing, contract renewals, Teams-based intake, SAP transition support')
Products: iSource, iSupplier, Merlin (Salesforce)
decision_criteria.narrative: Wanzl's evaluation scope is confirmed from the 18 May discovery call and Next Step agenda: (1) supplier onboarding across 20 countries and 11 ERP systems, (2) RFX and auction platform for negotiation leverage, (3) operational purchasing / intake (Teams-based intake mentioned), (4) AI-led sourcing and contract renewals (Merlin positioning), (5) SAP transition support (current SAP contract expiring, migration underway). Manuel Kehle stated 'we need a platform that unifies RFX and supplier onboarding without forcing us to rip-and-replace all 11 ERPs at once,' signaling integration flexibility as a criterion. However, formal RFP criteria, scoring weights, and use-case validation (e.g. test cases, proof-of-concept, technical deep-dive) have not been run yet. The deep-dive session is the milestone to validate and refine these criteria with IT, operational procurement, and Group Purchasing stakeholders.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

items.trigger: 42 days since last buyer contact (18 May call), 7 days since rep scheduling request (22 Jun Next Step) with no buyer reply. Risk of falling out of shortlist before year-end gate.
items.expected_effect: Restart engagement, confirm deep-dive within 2-3 weeks, prevent shortlist drop-off.

items.action: Multi-thread into IT and operational procurement: during deep-dive scheduling outreach (rank-1 move), request IT lead and operational procurement lead names/contact info from Manuel or Siegfried. Initiate direct pre-deep-dive calls with IT (to validate 11-ERP integration approach and SAP transition support) and operational procurement (to validate RFX/auction and supplier-onboarding workflows for direct-spend). Build relationships before cross-functional deep-dive.
items.trigger: Single-threaded (only Manuel Kehle engaged). 18 May call confirmed need for IT and operational procurement validation ('we need to bring in operational procurement and IT to validate the integration story'). No IT or operational procurement contacts in Salesforce Contact Roles.
items.expected_effect: Multi-thread before deep-dive, de-risk single-point-of-contact dependency, build IT and operational procurement sponsorship.

items.action: Map and engage Economic Buyer: ask Manuel Kehle and Siegfried Schmidt during deep-dive scheduling and session who controls budget approval for the €200k, 20-country platform initiative (CFO, CIO, Group CPO, or steering-board chair). Request introduction to EB for post-deep-dive executive briefing on business case, ROI, and strategic fit. Position EB engagement as critical before year-end beauty-contest shortlist.
items.trigger: Economic Buyer unmapped. 18 May call referenced 'steering board' for approval, but no individual named. €200k, multi-year, 20-country scope requires C-level budget authority.
items.expected_effect: Map EB, secure executive sponsorship, de-risk budget authority gap before shortlist gate.

items.action: Build quantified value case for deep-dive: prepare ROI model showing cost of fragmented procurement (11 ERPs, regional process duplication, lost negotiation leverage) vs Zycus unified platform. Estimate savings potential (e.g. 10-15% procurement spend reduction via consolidated RFX/auctions, headcount efficiency via automated supplier onboarding, cycle-time reduction). Present in deep-dive session and validate assumptions with operational procurement and Group Purchasing. Anchor business case before beauty-contest stage.
items.trigger: No quantified value case yet. Manuel Kehle articulated qualitative pain ('we lose negotiation leverage because we cannot consolidate RFX across geographies') on 18 May, but no savings target, headcount impact, or cycle-time improvement discussed.
items.expected_effect: Quantify business case, strengthen EB pitch, differentiate vs competitors in RFP evaluation.

items.action: Surface competitive intel and position Merlin AI differentiation: during deep-dive session, ask Wanzl who else is in the year-end beauty-contest shortlist (SAP Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua, or others). Understand their positioning and Wanzl's perception. Position Zycus differentiation: (1) Merlin AI-led sourcing and contract renewals as strategic advantage for AI-hungry enterprise buyers, (2) 11-ERP integration flexibility (no rip-and-replace), (3) 20-country supplier-onboarding scale. Make competitive positioning explicit before RFP scoring.
items.trigger: Competition unknown. 18 May call referenced 'year-end beauty contest' RFP, confirming multi-vendor evaluation, but no competitor names surfaced. Competitive intel blind spot.
items.expected_effect: Surface competitors, position Merlin AI and integration differentiation, strengthen RFP positioning.

north_star_verdict.headline: 42-day buyer silence in early Qualified stage with no momentum toward RFP positioning. Rep reached out 22 Jun (7 days ago) to schedule deep-dive, awaiting buyer confirmation. Multi-threading critical before year-end beauty-contest gate.

flags: Single-threaded: only Manuel Kehle (Expert Methods & Processes, Evaluator) actively engaged; Siegfried Schmidt (VP Group Purchasing, Decision Maker contact role but functionally RFP process owner) present on 18 May call but not re-engaged since. No IT, operational procurement, or C-level stakeholders engaged yet despite governance structure (steering board, international content team, IT fully embedded). Multi-threading is critical to survive the RFP shortlist gate (Jun/Jul 2026): secure the deep-dive session and use it to engage IT, operational procurement, and map the Economic Buyer.

Economic Buyer unmapped: no CFO, CIO, or Group CPO identified or engaged. Siegfried Schmidt mentioned board budget approval for 2026 and steering-board governance, but the executive sponsor who will sign the €200k contract is unknown. For a multi-year, 20-country procurement-platform initiative, EB engagement is mandatory before the beauty-contest stage (year-end 2026). Next move: ask Manuel or Siegfried directly during deep-dive session: 'Who will approve the budget and sign the contract, and when can we present the ROI case to them?'

Fit gap on direct-spend document management must be addressed in deep-dive session: Wanzl's current three-pillar scope prioritizes direct/operational procurement (supplier onboarding, RFX, operational purchasing), but Zycus explicitly does NOT handle direct-material production planning, 8D quality, or product-level document management/technical drawings. Wanzl is open to best-of-breed approach, but Zycus must either (a) validate that RFX/supplier-onboarding modules are generic enough for direct-spend workflows, or (b) position best-of-breed (Zycus for indirect, another platform for direct) and acknowledge the trade-off. Make Merlin/AI agents (autonomous sourcing, contract renewals, Teams intake) the centerpiece differentiator that offsets the direct-spend gap and that no legacy S2P platform can match.

Single-threaded: only Manuel Kehle (Evaluator) engaged; no IT, operational procurement, Siegfried Schmidt (Decision Maker), or C-level stakeholders contacted. Multi-threading critical for cross-functional deep-dive and RFP shortlist survival.
Economic Buyer unmapped: no CFO, CIO, or Group CPO identified for €200k, 20-country platform initiative requiring steering-board approval.
No quantified value case: pain confirmed (fragmented procurement, 11 ERPs, lost negotiation leverage) but no savings target, headcount reduction, or cycle-time improvement articulated.
Competitive intel blind spot: 'year-end beauty contest' RFP confirmed, but no competitor names surfaced.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Wanzl's scope (supplier onboarding, RFX/auctions, operational purchasing, 11-ERP integration, AI-led sourcing, Teams-based intake, SAP transition support) aligns with iSource/iSupplier/Merlin platform. One unresolved fit gap: Wanzl's current procurement scope includes direct-spend (production materials), and Zycus does NOT handle direct-material production planning, 8D quality reports, or product-level document management. Must validate in deep-dive that RFX/supplier-onboarding modules are generic enough for direct-spend workflows, or position best-of-breed (indirect/strategic sourcing) and acknowledge trade-off.

hard.ais_why:
* AI capabilities were rep-presented within an active evaluation: Zycus highlighted "AI-driven autonomous sourcing, contract renewal, and supplier management agents" (e.g., Heineken autonomous sourcing up to EUR 20,000) and a Teams "digital assistant."
* But the customer's stated priorities are integration and scope, not AI: a middleware to connect "11 ERP systems," supplier onboarding/sourcing/operational purchasing across ~20 countries, and concerns about "direct material procurement" coverage.
* It is a structured, methodical selection (screening mid-year, deep dives, year-end "beauty contest," project start late 2026); AI is one capability under evaluation, not a voiced driver. Medium confidence.

deltas.to: Cooling engagement: 42 days since last two-way buyer touch (18 May call), 7 days since rep outreach (22 Jun) with no buyer reply. Risk of falling out of shortli
deltas.subject: Cooling engagement: 42 days since last two-way buyer touch (18 May call), 7 days since rep outreach (22 Jun) with no buyer reply. Risk of falling out of shortlist before year-end gate.

Momentum stalling: 39 days since last two-way buyer engagement (18 May call), 4 days since rep outreach (22 Jun) with no buyer confirmation of deep-dive session. Risk of falling out of RFP shortlist before Jun/Jul gate if engagement does not resume soon.

Single-threaded: only Manuel Kehle (Expert Methods & Processes, Evaluator) actively engaged; Siegfried Schmidt (VP Group Purchasing, Decision Maker contact role but functionally RFP process owner) present on 18 May call but not re-engaged since. No IT, operational procurement, or C-level stakeholders engaged yet despite governance structure (steering board, international content team, IT fully embedded). Multi-threading is critical to survive the RFP shortlist gate (Jun/Jul 2026): secure the deep-dive session and use it to engage IT, operational procurement, and map the Economic Buyer.

Economic Buyer unmapped: no CFO, CIO, or Group CPO identified or engaged. Siegfried Schmidt mentioned board budget approval for 2026 and steering-board governance, but the executive sponsor who will sign the €200k contract is unknown. For a multi-year, 20-country procurement-platform initiative, EB engagement is mandatory before the beauty-contest stage (year-end 2026). Next move: ask Manuel or Siegfried directly during deep-dive session: 'Who will approve the budget and sign the contract, and when can we present the ROI case to them?'

Fit gap on direct-spend document management must be addressed in deep-dive session: Wanzl's current three-pillar scope prioritizes direct/operational procurement (supplier onboarding, RFX, operational purchasing), but Zycus explicitly does NOT handle direct-material production planning, 8D quality, or product-level document management/technical drawings. Wanzl is open to best-of-breed approach, but Zycus must either (a) validate that RFX/supplier-onboarding modules are generic enough for direct-spend workflows, or (b) position best-of-breed (Zycus for indirect, another platform for direct) and acknowledge the trade-off. Make Merlin/AI agents (autonomous sourcing, contract renewals, Teams intake) the centerpiece differentiator that offsets the direct-spend gap and that no legacy S2P platform can match.
